assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends Extended release channel and explains it provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for Regular/Stable, with control plane minor versions not auto-upgraded

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows the command '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and explains this allows security patches on control plane but blocks node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Response configures maintenance window for Saturday 2-8 AM with 6-hour duration using '--maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA"'

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Response creates separate node pools: 'h100-training' for training workloads and 'h100-inference' for inference workloads, with different configurations and upgrade tolerances

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Response shows '--enable-autoupgrade=false' for training node pools and provides detailed workflow for manual upgrades during training gaps, including removing and re-applying exclusions

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Response includes extended termination grace period (3600 seconds) for checkpointing and shows operational workflow that includes 'Checkpoint training state' before maintenance

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on 'GPU Driver Validation' with specific commands to create staging node pools and test driver compatibility before production upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Compact placement policy ensures RDMA topology is preserved during upgrades' and includes RDMA networking considerations in the GPU-specific section
